Build the sample directly from the raw byte slice instead — no char-boundary
constraint needed since ANT is a byte-level model.

commit.message:
Funnel visualization: derive from averaged rates over selected time range

Instead of using fixed last-month snapshot values, funnel now:
1. Averages the rate trend data over selected time range (3/6/12M)
2. Applies SKU profile multipliers to those averaged rates
3. Computes absolute volumes from visits × averaged rate

So Paid % over 12M shows ~4.1% (avg of 3.2%-5.0%) matching the
line chart, not 5.0% (last month only).

commit.message:
[a11y] Fix `@elastic/eui/icon-accessibility-rules` violations across 55 kibana-presentation files (#260694)

Closes: https://github.com/elastic/kibana/issues/260637

Resolves 88 ESLint violations of `@elastic/eui/icon-accessibility-rules`
across 55 files owned by `@elastic/kibana-presentation`. Every `EuiIcon`
must have either an accessible label (`aria-label`, `title`, or
`aria-labelledby`) or be marked `aria-hidden={true}` if decorative.

## Strategy

**`aria-hidden={true}`** — applied to the majority of icons that are
decorative: drag handles inside labeled containers, icons in context
menu items (where the `name` field provides the label), icons alongside
visible text, and status icons inside tooltipped wrappers.

```tsx
// before
<EuiIcon type="grabHorizontal" />

// after — parent div already carries aria-label
<EuiIcon type="grabHorizontal" aria-hidden={true} />
```

**`aria-label` via `i18n.translate()`** — applied to icons that
independently convey meaning or are interactive:

| File | Icon | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `presentation_panel_title.tsx` | `info` with `tabIndex={0}` |
`aria-label="Description"` |
| `error_component.tsx` | `warning` with `onClick` | `aria-label="View
error details"` |
| `asset_picker.tsx` | `checkInCircleFilled` (selected state) |
`aria-label="Selected"` |
| `saved_query_management_list.tsx` | `info` / `clock` metadata
indicators | `aria-label="Has description"` / `aria-label="Has time
filter"` |

**`aria-label` on parent + `aria-hidden` on icon** —
`page_manager.component.tsx` had an unlabeled native `<button>` whose
only content was an icon; added `aria-label` to the button and
`aria-hidden={true}` to the icon.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: alexwizp <20072247+alexwizp@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(e2e): add CREDITOR_SERVICE_URL to debt-service in docker-compose

The debt-service was missing CREDITOR_SERVICE_URL in its Docker
environment, causing it to use the default http://localhost:8092 —
which inside the Docker network points to the container itself, not
the creditor-service container.

This caused validateCreditorAction() to fail with a connection error
on every claim submission, triggering the Resilience4j circuit breaker
fallback that threw OpenDebtException(CRITICAL), which GlobalExceptionHandler
mapped to HTTP 503. The creditor-portal then showed AFVIST with
'503: Debt service unavailable', failing FR-P012-02b.

Fix: add CREDITOR_SERVICE_URL: http://creditor-service:8092 alongside
the already-correct CASE_SERVICE_URL pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

This commit resolves major architectural goals by hardening execution stability,
improving text-extraction heuristics, and enforcing privacy-by-default routing:

2. AST-Based Semantic Pruning

     AST calculations via the `tl` crate (`evaluate_block_saturation`).
   - Accurately computes link density by evaluating structural DOM nodes,
     significantly improving the accuracy of sidebar and navigation pruning.

3. Privacy by Default
   - Added an explicit `--allow-cloud` CLI flag to gate third-party
     extraction proxies (Jina.ai).

fix: align DepthRenderer readiness check with rendering logic (#18266)

## Problem

`DepthRenderer.customIsReadyFunction` checks `isReady()` for all
submeshes, but `DepthRenderer.isReady()` returns `false` for materials
with `disableDepthWrite`. The actual `renderSubMesh` function skips
those submeshes entirely. This mismatch causes `scene.whenReadyAsync()`
to never resolve when any mesh in the scene has `disableDepthWrite =
true`.

Forum report: https://forum.babylonjs.com/t/63104

## Fix

Added the same skip conditions from `renderSubMesh` (no material,
`infiniteDistance`, `disableDepthWrite`, zero vertices) as `continue`
guards in `customIsReadyFunction`.

## Tests

Added unit tests in
`packages/dev/core/test/unit/Rendering/babylon.depthRenderer.test.ts`.

Fix json_quote dropping ByteConcat tail when quoting (#7)

## Problem

`json_quote` silently truncates regexes built from string literals
longer than 31 bytes (`MAX_BYTE_CONCAT`) when those literals contain
characters that need JSON escaping (`\n`, `\r`, `\t`, `\\\\`, `"`,
control chars, `\x7F`).

Long literals are internally split into chained `ByteConcat(bytes,
tail)` nodes. When `json_quote` encounters a chunk whose bytes include a
character needing quoting, it splits and re-encodes those bytes — but
drops `args[0]` (the mapped tail), discarding everything after that
chunk.

The `else` branches correctly preserve the tail; only the quoting branch
was missing it.

Reported in guidance-ai/llguidance#326.

## Fix

Append `args[0]` (the mapped tail expression) to `acc` before calling
`_mk_concat_vec`, matching the behavior of the other branches.

## Test

Added `test_json_quote_long_literal_with_newline` — a 78-byte literal
with `\n` at byte offset 43. Before the fix, the json-quoted regex fails
to match the correctly escaped output. After the fix, it matches.

All existing tests continue to pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: skip JIT execution in TailCall handler (compile-only)

TailCall is a terminal opcode that Maglev/Turbofan emit Deoptimize for.
Attempting try_execute_best_jit on every tail-call iteration corrupts
thread-local state via deopt side-effects and produces wrong results
for recursive tail-call functions.

Keep compilation triggers so JIT code is ready for function entry
(run_dispatch), but skip execution during tail calls.

feat: inner-step training loop — 8 gradient passes per trie read/write cycle

Phases B and C now amortise trie I/O across INNER_STEPS (default 8) mini-batch
gradient passes before performing one trie write. This keeps the GPU busy for
the full inner loop while a single trie read/write pair is shared across all
inner steps.

Changes:
- config.rs: add INNER_STEPS = 8 constant with lr-scaling note
- engine.rs: add read_for_encode(), forward_with_mem(), write_hidden()
  — separates trie I/O from forward compute so callers can interleave freely
- train.rs: add inner_steps field to TrainConfig; rewrite phase_b/phase_c
  outer loop = one trie read → inner loop (inner_steps fresh mini-batches,
  accumulate scaled losses) → one optimizer.backward_step → one trie write
- main.rs: add --inner-steps CLI flag

Effective batch = batch_b * inner_steps (e.g. 8 * 8 = 64 now, 512 * 8 = 4096
on Colab with --batch-b 512 --inner-steps 8).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace jscalendar 1.0 with Flatpickr compatibility shim (#1406)

* feat: replace jscalendar 1.0 with flatpickr compatibility shim

Replace the abandoned jscalendar 1.0 date picker library (~1841 lines)
with a thin compatibility shim that delegates to Flatpickr 4.6.13.

The shim approach (suggested by @phc007 in #1355) avoids modifying 99+
JSP files: Calendar.setup() calls are intercepted and translated to
flatpickr() initialisations automatically.

Changes:
- calendar.js: Replaced with shim that defines Calendar global stubs,
  auto-loads flatpickr.min.js, and queues Calendar.setup() calls
- calendar-setup.js: Replaced with Calendar.setup() → flatpickr adapter
  (format conversion, button triggers, time support, locale, callbacks)
- calendar.css: Now imports flatpickr.min.css
- lang/calendar-{en,fr,es,pl,pt}.js: Simplified to set backward-compat
  properties plus Calendar._flatpickrLocale for the shim
- library/flatpickr/l10n/{fr,es,pl,pt}.js: Added flatpickr locale files
- Removed unused jscalendar files (skins, docs, demos, PHP, images)

Fixes #1355
